key_insight: Outlier-free isogeometric analysis with divergence-conforming spline spaces preserves stability and enables order-independent explicit time stepping for poroelastic wave propagation.
engineering_relevance: This makes higher-order poroelastic simulations more accurate and efficient, with larger stable time steps and fewer degrees of freedom than classical finite elements.
method: Compare primal and reduced poroelastic formulations
Use divergence-conforming spline spaces for matrix and fluid displacements
Perform spectral analysis and 1D/2D timestep scaling tests
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical proof and spectral/benchmark analysis showing fast and slow wave branches and timestep scaling that is essentially independent of polynomial order when outlier modes are removed.
watch_item: The benefits depend on careful treatment of outlier modes and conforming spline discretization, which may add implementation complexity.
paper_title: Isogeometric Analysis for Explicit Wave Propagation in Poroelastic Media
